info: Choose which optimization tools to enable (recommended: all).

rtk.enabled:
## RTK

Prefix ALL CLI commands with `rtk` (e.g. `rtk git diff`, `rtk pnpm test`). Read-only commands like `cat`, `ls`, `Get-Content` are exempt. If `rtk` is not available, report blocker and stop CLI execution.
rtk.disabled: RTK was not selected during onboarding. Do not assume `rtk` exists.

caveman.enabled:
## Caveman

Caveman mode active every response. Load `@caveman` skill for full guidance. No revert unless user says "stop caveman" or "normal mode".
caveman.disabled: Caveman was not selected during onboarding. Use normal concise style.

codegraph.enabled:
## CodeGraph

CodeGraph is an MCP server, NOT a CLI tool. Do NOT run `codegraph` in bash. Use `codegraph_search` for symbol lookups and `codegraph_impact` for blast-radius checks in the main session. NEVER call `codegraph_explore` directly — it returns large payloads. ALWAYS spawn an Explore sub-agent for exploration questions, and include the codegraph MCP instructions in its spawn prompt.
codegraph.disabled: Codegraph was not selected during onboarding. Use standard grep/glob/read for code exploration.

memory.enabled:
## Basic Memory

Basic Memory is an MCP server, NOT a CLI tool. Do NOT run `basic-memory` in bash. Use `search` to find prior decisions or context before implementing unfamiliar areas. Use `write_note` to store architecture decisions, resolved ambiguities, and cross-agent context (title: `task-<id>-result` or `exploration-<topic>`). When spawning sub-agents, include the basic-memory MCP instructions in their prompt so they know it exists.

rtk.enabled: Prefix all bash commands with `rtk` when RTK is enabled.

codegraph.enabled: Use codegraph MCP tools (NOT CLI commands). Do NOT run `codegraph` in bash — use the MCP tools directly: `codegraph_search`, `codegraph_impact`, `codegraph_callers`, `codegraph_callees`, `codegraph_node`.

memory.enabled: Use basic-memory MCP tools (NOT CLI commands). Do NOT run `basic-memory` in bash — use the MCP tools directly: `write_note`, `edit_note`, `search`, `build_context`, `recent_activity`.
